title: Paya Soup

seo.title: Paya Soup Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Goat Trotters Soup
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best authentic Paya Soup with our easy Pakistani recipe. This flavorful goat trotters soup is perfect for a comforting meal. Get the step-by-step guide!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Pakistani Paya Soup garnished with fresh ginger, green chilies, and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, aromatic flavors of our authentic Pakistani Paya Soup, a comforting and hearty dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Paya Soup is a rich and gelatinous Pakistani delicacy made from slow-cooked goat or lamb trotters. This intensely flavorful and aromatic soup is renowned for its tender meat and a thick, spicy broth, offering a truly comforting culinary experience. It is often enjoyed as a hearty breakfast or a special occasion meal, celebrated for its unique texture and deeply satisfying taste.

ingredients.amount: 2-3 green cardamom, 1 black cardamom, 2-3 cloves, 1 cinnamon stick

ingredients.notes: mixed with 1/2 cup water for slurry (optional thickener)

instructions.title: Prepare the Trotters
instructions.text: Ensure the goat or lamb trotters are thoroughly cleaned, washed, and any remaining hair is singed off. You can ask your butcher to do this. Pat them dry before cooking.

instructions.title: Sauté Onions and Spices
instructions.text: In a large heavy-bottomed pot or pressure cooker, heat oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and fry until golden brown. Then add the whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon) and ginger garlic paste. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Sear Trotters and Cook Masala
instructions.text: Add the cleaned trotters to the pot and sear them on all sides for about 5-7 minutes until lightly browned. Now, add the red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring continuously.

instructions.title: Add Yogurt and Simmer
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low, add the whisked yogurt, and mix thoroughly. Cook the masala with the trotters for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the masala. This step is crucial for developing deep flavor.

instructions.title: Slow Cook the Paya
instructions.text: Pour in 8-10 cups of hot water, ensuring the trotters are fully submerged. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for at least 3.5 to 4 hours (or 45-60 minutes in a pressure cooker after the first whistle) until the trotters are extremely tender and the meat starts to fall off the bone. Skim off any excess fat if desired.

instructions.title: Thicken and Finish
instructions.text: If you prefer a thicker gravy, mix 2 tablespoons of wheat flour with 1/2 cup of water to create a smooth slurry. Gradually add this slurry to the simmering paya, stirring constantly to avoid lumps, and cook for another 10-15 minutes until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. Adjust salt if necessary.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the paya is cooked to perfection, transfer it to a serving bowl. Garnish generously with julienned fresh ginger, sliced green chilies, and chopped fresh cilantro. Serve hot with naan, tandoori roti, or plain rice.

tips: For truly tender Paya, slow cooking for an extended period is key. If using a pressure cooker, ensure sufficient water to prevent drying out and achieve maximum gelatin extraction.

Always sear the trotters properly before adding water; this locks in flavor and contributes to a richer broth. Don't rush the browning of onions either, as it forms the base of the gravy's color and taste.
Paya often tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, reheating gently on the stovetop.
article: Paya Soup is a beloved Pakistani Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
